requirements.title: Create study protocol draft
requirements.statement: A sponsor manager can create a Study with title, phase, indication, planned sites, and planned subjects. The system stores the protocol draft and emits an audit entry.

requirements.title: Approve protocol
requirements.statement: A protocol in draft can be approved by a regulatory officer and PI. The status transitions to approved, a ProtocolVersion is created, and downstream site activation is enabled.

requirements.title: Amend active study
requirements.statement: A sponsor manager can amend an active study. A new ProtocolVersion is created, sites are notified, and enrolled subjects are flagged for re-consent if required.

requirements.title: Milestone dashboard
requirements.statement: The dashboard shows actual vs planned milestone dates and alerts on overdue milestones.

requirements.title: Add site
requirements.statement: A sponsor manager can add a site with PI, address, and regulatory contacts. The site is created in pending status and a SiteActivationChecklist is generated.

requirements.title: Activate site
requirements.statement: A regulatory officer can activate a site once all checklist items are complete. The site status transitions to activated and an audit entry is recorded.

requirements.title: Suspend or close site
requirements.statement: A sponsor manager or safety officer can suspend or close an activated site. No new enrolments are allowed, scheduled visits are flagged, and the reason is audit-logged.

requirements.title: Monitoring visit
requirements.statement: A CRA can log a monitoring visit with findings and CAPA. The visit is linked to the site and queries may be raised.

requirements.title: Register potential subject
requirements.statement: A CRC can register a potential subject with demographics and screening data. The system checks inclusion/exclusion criteria and produces eligible, ineligible, or pending_review.

requirements.title: Enrol subject after consent
requirements.statement: An eligible subject at an activated site can be enrolled after informed consent is recorded. A unique subject number is assigned and a baseline visit is scheduled.

requirements.title: Re-consent after amendment
requirements.statement: When an amendment affects enrolled subjects, the system flags them. The CRC records re-consent with the new version; subjects not re-consented are suspended from further IP dispensing.

requirements.title: Withdraw subject
requirements.statement: An enrolled subject can withdraw consent or be discontinued by the investigator. The withdrawal reason and date are recorded, future visits are cancelled, and a withdrawal-of-consent document is generated.

requirements.title: Randomise subject
requirements.statement: The system can randomise a subject in a two-arm double-blind study to a stratified arm without revealing treatment mapping to unblinded users.

requirements.title: Schedule visit
requirements.statement: A visit scheduled from the study's visit template computes the visit window and creates an Appointment plus a SubjectVisit.

requirements.title: Record visit conduct
requirements.statement: Recording actual date, assessments, and findings transitions the SubjectVisit status to completed, missed, or early_terminated, and auto-flags deviations outside the visit window.

requirements.title: Flag missed visits
requirements.statement: A nightly task flags scheduled visits that pass window_max_date without occurrence, raises a missed-visit alert, and creates a ProtocolDeviation if required.

requirements.title: CRA verification
requirements.statement: A CRA can mark a visit's key data points as verified or query-raised. The query is linked to the subject and visit, and the monitoring status is updated.

requirements.title: Upload regulatory document
requirements.statement: A regulatory officer can upload a document with type, version, expiry, and optional site. It is stored with a DocumentReference, eTMF folder path, and audit entry.

requirements.title: eTMF dashboard
requirements.statement: The eTMF dashboard shows per-folder completeness and flags expired documents.

requirements.title: Ethics expiry alert
requirements.statement: Ethics approval expiry within 60 days raises an alert and blocks site activation if expiry passes.

requirements.title: Adverse event reporting deadline
requirements.statement: An adverse event with onset, severity, seriousness, and causality triggers computation of the regulatory reporting deadline (7- or 15-day for SUSAR), assigns a safety officer, and emits an audit entry.

requirements.title: SUSAR deadline alert
requirements.statement: A SUSAR within 24 hours of deadline or past deadline raises critical alerts to the safety officer, PI, and sponsor manager.

requirements.title: Receive IP shipment
requirements.statement: A site pharmacist can record receipt of an IP shipment with lot, expiry, quantity, and storage condition. Inventory is incremented and a receipt is audit-logged.

requirements.title: Dispense IP
requirements.statement: Dispensing IP to an enrolled subject decrements inventory, creates an IpDispense record, and notifies pharmacy-system / eps if the IP is tracked there.

requirements.title: Define budget categories
requirements.statement: A sponsor manager can define budget categories (start-up, per-subject, procedure, pass-through). The planned budget is stored and actuals accumulate from enrolment and visit events.

requirements.title: Generate invoice
requirements.statement: A finance coordinator can generate a draft invoice from actuals, route it to the sponsor, and link it to supporting events.

requirements.title: Recruitment dashboard
requirements.statement: The recruitment dashboard shows actual vs planned enrolment by site, screen-fail reasons, and dropout rates.

requirements.title: Register agentic subject
requirements.statement: An agent owner can register an AgentSubject with principal_id, persona_key, superpersona_contract_id, model_version, and AgentAttestation trust bundle. The system validates the attestation and emits an audit entry.

requirements.title: Agent consent contract
requirements.statement: An owner accepting the AgentConsentContract makes the agent eligible for enrolment in agent-in-the-loop studies.

requirements.title: Dashboard query response time
requirements.statement: The system shall return recruitment and milestone dashboard queries within 500 ms at the 95th percentile under normal load.

requirements.title: Concurrent enrolment throughput
requirements.statement: The system shall support at least 50 concurrent subject enrolments per minute without error or response-time degradation beyond 1 second.

requirements.title: OIDC JWT authentication
requirements.statement: The system shall authenticate every API request with a valid OIDC JWT and reject requests with missing, expired, or tampered tokens.

requirements.title: Role-based access control
requirements.statement: The system shall enforce role-based access control so that sponsor managers, regulatory officers, safety officers, and coordinators can only perform actions permitted by their roles.

requirements.title: Audit log durability
requirements.statement: The system shall durably record every protocol status change, site activation, subject enrolment, and adverse-event report to an append-only audit log.

requirements.title: SUSAR critical alert latency
requirements.statement: The system shall raise a critical SUSAR alert to the safety officer, PI, and sponsor manager within 60 seconds of adverse-event creation when the regulatory deadline is within 24 hours.

requirements.title: FHIR R4 export compatibility
requirements.statement: The system shall export study, site, subject, and adverse-event data as valid FHIR R4 resources for interoperability with external research platforms.

requirements.title: Configurable workflow versioning
requirements.statement: The system shall support configurable study workflow and consent versioning without requiring a code deployment for routine protocol amendments.
